---
title: "Multiclass ODA (C >= 3) with oda_fit()"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
---

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>")
```

**Purpose:** Introduce multiclass ODA (`oda_fit()` with C ≥ 3 classes). Cover
the K-class chance benchmark (100/K, not 50%), Mean PAC, ESS, per-class PAC,
ordered multiclass cut values and segment-class assignments, and categorical
multiclass. Use the iris and protein examples.

**Source material:**
- `tests/testthat/test-iris.R` — iris Petal.Length K=3 gold values
- `vignettes/protein-type-multiclass-oda.Rmd` — K=4 categorical example

**Eval policy:** iris live (mcarlo = FALSE, instant). Protein live with
mc_iter = 500, mc_seed = 42. Canonical iris and protein MC results stated as
text.

---

*This article is not yet expanded. Placeholder sections are listed below.*

## Multiclass ODA: C ≥ 3

## The K-class chance benchmark

## Ordered multiclass: cut values and segment-class assignments

### iris example

## Categorical multiclass: level-class mapping

### protein type example

## Interpreting per-class PAC at K > 2

## Notes on LOO for multiclass
